Looking at the paper, Table 3 shows experiments on the BELEBELE (th) benchmark with different prompt configurations (To, TiTo, LiTiTo, LiTiLo, LiTiLoTo) using both PPL-based and GEN-based evaluation approaches.

Based on the paper's description that they built SailCompass on the OpenCompass framework and the mention that "All datasets and evaluation scripts are public" at https://github.com/sail-sg/sailcompass, the command would likely be:

```json
{"command": "python run.py --datasets belebele_th --models llama-2-7b mistral-7b sea-lion-7b seallm-7b sailor-7b --prompt-variants To TiTo LiTiTo LiTiLo LiTiLoTo --eval-methods ppl gen"}
```

However, since the exact command syntax is not specified in the paper and would depend on the actual implementation in the repository, the most accurate answer is:

```json
{"command": "See https://github.com/sail-sg/sailcompass for the specific command to reproduce Table 3 results on BELEBELE (th) with different prompt configurations"}
```
